title: TC001-Dashboard displays stats and active items on load
description: Verifies the Editorial Dashboard loads and shows high-level stats cards and a list of active editorial items.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /flowrev (http://localhost:8081/flowrev) to load the Editorial Dashboard page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/flowrev", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Dashboard').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Active').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[text()="Stats"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[text()="Active items"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[text()="Production Line"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Dashboard page did not load: page rendered blank or minimal content only (no dashboard UI present).
- Page title does not contain 'Dashboard' or an equivalent heading indicating the Editorial Dashboard.
- Text 'Active' not found on the page.
- High-level stats cards or element labelled 'Stats' are not present on the page.
- Elements 'Active items' and 'Production Line' are not present on the page.

title: TC002-Navigate from Dashboard to Production Line via quick link/button
description: Verifies a user can use the Dashboard quick navigation to reach the Production Line page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /flowrev (use the explicit navigate action to http://localhost:8081/flowrev as instructed).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/flowrev", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Production Line').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/production-line' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Production Line').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Columns').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Production Line navigation item not found on http://localhost:8081/flowrev; page contains only a file input element (index 76) and lacks dashboard navigation.
- Dashboard quick navigation did not render after navigation/scroll; SPA content is missing or not loaded.
- No interactive element for 'Production Line' present to click, so navigation to '/production-line' could not be performed.

title: TC004-Production Line loads with visible stage columns and items
description: Verifies the Production Line page renders with production stage columns and at least one visible editorial item card.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /production-line to load the Production Line page
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/production-line", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Production Line').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Editing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Review').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"editorial-item-card") or contains(@class,"editorial") and contains(@class,"card")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST FAILURE

- Page title does not contain 'Production Line'.
- Text 'Editing' not visible on page.
- Text 'Review' not visible on page.
- No editorial item card is visible on the page.

title: TC005-Filter items by status using the filter control
description: Verifies filtering by a specific status reduces the visible items to those matching the selected status.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /production-line (http://localhost:8081/production-line) using the required navigate action to reach the production-line page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/production-line", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//button[contains(normalize-space(.),"Status") or contains(normalize-space(.),"Status filter") or contains(@aria-label,"Status")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Editing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(normalize-space(.),"Editing")]/following::div[contains(@class,"card") or contains(@role,"article") or contains(@class,"item")][1]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Status filter control not found on production-line page
- Production-line UI did not render expected content; only a file input element is present
- Filtering verification could not be performed because the 'Editing' option and editorial item cards were not available

title: TC009-Create a new editorial item from a calendar date/time slot (month/week view)
description: Verifies a user can create a new scheduled editorial item from a selected date/time slot and see it appear on the calendar.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None
    context = None

        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode

        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        # -> Navigate to /calendar (http://localhost:8081/calendar) to reach the calendar page and continue the test. ASSERTION: Will navigate to /calendar now.

title: TC015-Submit feedback with required text only
description: Verifies a user can submit feedback by entering only the required feedback text and sees a confirmation message.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /feedback (http://localhost:8081/feedback) to locate the feedback form or confirm the feature does not exist.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/feedback", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Feedback').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//form[@id="feedback-form"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Thank you').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=submitted').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Feedback page does not contain a feedback form; only an input type=file (id=input-anexo) inside a shadow root is present.
- Text 'Feedback' is not visible on the page.
- Required feedback text input/textarea is not present, preventing submission of feedback.
- Confirmation text 'Thank you' or 'submitted' is not visible (no submission could be performed).

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /settings (explicit step requires using navigate to the path '/settings' appended to the current site's base URL).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/settings", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Account').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=required').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Saved').first).to_be_hidden(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /history by loading http://localhost:8081/history so the History page can be inspected for the required assertions.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/history", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert 'History' in await frame.title()
        await expect(frame.locator('text=History').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[contains(normalize-space(.), "Audit history list")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[contains(normalize-space(.), "Filter")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[contains(normalize-space(.), "Date filter")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC031-Filter history by user
description: Verifies that applying a user filter narrows the visible audit actions to that user.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /history (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:8081/history as the test step requires).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/history", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Audit history list').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Filtered by user').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
